subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from provided rulebook excerpt)
- **Supported count** — 2-4 players.
- **2 players** — Cattle market display starts with 7 market cattle cards.
- **3 players** — Cattle market display starts with 10 market cattle cards.
- **4 players** — Cattle market display starts with 13 market cattle cards.
- **2/4 players** — Domino-row equivalent in setup context: draw 4 tiles where rule says same as kings in play (GWT equivalent appears in job market rows and market display counts).
- **3/4 players** — Each player uses a player-count tile to cover Phase A on player board (2-player does not).
- **Setup scaling table** — Job market initially contains between 3 and 7 worker tiles depending on player count.
- **Starting money scales** — 1st player $6, 2nd $7, 3rd $8 (if any), 4th $9 (if any).
- **Movement fee matrix scales** — Green/black hand fee values differ in 2p, 3p, and 4p games.

#### Core Boards & Tokens
- **Game board** — 1.
- **Player boards** — 4.
- **Player count tiles** — 4 (double-sided: 3-player side / 4-player side).
- **Cattlemen** — 4 (1 per player).
- **Player discs** — 56 (14 per player).
- **Engines** — 4 (1 per player).
- **Certificate markers** — 4 (1 per player).

#### Tile Groups
- **Worker tiles** — 54 total (18 cowboy, 18 craftsman, 18 engineer).
- **Hazard tiles** — 18 total (6 flood, 6 drought, 6 rockfall).
- **Teepee tiles** — 22 total (11 blue, 11 green).
- **Back-number system** — Worker, hazard, and teepee tiles are marked 1/2/3 on backs.

#### Cards
- **Cattle cards total** — 92.
- **Player cattle cards** — 56 total (14 per player).
- **Market cattle cards** — 36 total.
- **Objective cards** — 24.
- **Starting objective cards** — 4.

#### Building Tiles
- **Building tiles total** — 47.
- **Neutral building tiles** — 7 (A-G).
- **Private building tiles** — 40 (10 per player, double-sided a/b).

#### Economy & Scoring Items
- **Station master tiles** — 5.
- **Coins total** — 55 (35x$1, 20x$5).
- **Job market token** — 1.
- **Scoring note pad** — 1.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Global Board Setup
1. **Main board** — Place game board in center.
2. **Station masters** — Shuffle 5 station master tiles and place 1 face up on each station master space.
3. **Job marker** — Place job market token on top dashed round space.
4. **Neutral buildings** — First game: place A-G on matching spaces; later games: shuffle and randomize.
5. **Kansas City supply** — Sort worker/teepee/hazard tiles by back number (1/2/3), shuffle each pile, place by Kansas City.
6. **Initial tile reveal** — Reveal back-1 tiles and place by type until 7 total tiles enter board sections.
7. **Foresight row** — Fill foresight spaces with two back-1, two back-2, and two back-3 tiles.
8. **Objective display** — Shuffle 24 objective cards, reveal 4 into general objective display.
9. **Bank** — Place coins within reach of all players.

#### Player-Count Setup Values
--- 2 Players ---
- **Job market fill** — Fill from 2-player start column to space left of job market token.
- **Cattle market display** — Reveal 7 market cattle cards.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Job market fill** — Fill from 3-player start column to space left of job market token.
- **Cattle market display** — Reveal 10 market cattle cards.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Job market fill** — Fill from 4-player start column to space left of job market token.
- **Cattle market display** — Reveal 13 market cattle cards.

#### Cattle Market Sort Order
- **Color order** — Arrange left-to-right as yellow, red, blue, brown, purple.
- **Note** — Not all colors must appear.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Player Board & Personal Components
1. **Choose color** — Take matching player board.
2. **Private buildings** — Take your 10 private building tiles (1-10, a/b sides).
3. **Disc placement** — Place 14 discs on colored spaces of your board (leave 2 gray spaces empty).
4. **Engine placement** — Place engine on railroad starting space (red building).
5. **Certificate placement** — Place certificate marker at 0 on certificate track.
6. **Cattleman** — Keep cattleman beside your board.
7. **Herd deck** — Take your 14 player cattle cards, shuffle as draw stack, draw 4 cards to hand.
8. **Starting objective** — Shuffle 4 starting objective cards, deal 1 face up to each player, return leftovers.

#### Player-Count Tile Use
--- 2 Players ---
- **Player-count tile** — 0 player-count tiles placed over Phase A (tile not used).
--- 3 Players ---
- **Player-count tile** — Use matching tile on 3-player side to cover Phase A.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Player-count tile** — Use matching tile on 4-player side to cover Phase A.

#### Private Building Side Setup
- **Learning setup** — All players set private buildings to a-side in ascending order.
- **Advanced setup** — One player randomizes each side; all players copy those same side choices; no deliberate flips afterward.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start Player & Cash
1. **Choose start player** — Determine first player.
2. **Distribute cash** — Give players starting money by seating order.
3. **Begin turns** — Play proceeds clockwise from first player.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Starting money** — First player $6, second player $7.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Starting money** — First player $6, second player $7, third player $8.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Starting money** — First player $6, second player $7, third player $8, fourth player $9.

#### Core Goal Reminder
- **Main loop** — Move from trail start to Kansas City repeatedly.
- **Primary economy** — Deliver cattle by train to cities for money and VP.
- **Long game** — Improve herd, staff, buildings, and rail position for endgame scoring.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Phase A: Move** — Move cattleman to another location along the trail.
2. **Phase B: Act** — Resolve the reached location’s action(s).
3. **Phase C: Refill hand** — Draw up to hand limit (starts at 4).

#### Phase A Movement Rules
- **Location definition** — Kansas City and any tile space are locations; empty trail spaces are not locations.
- **Step counting** — Each location passed/reached costs 1 step.
- **First turn exception** — First turn only: place cattleman on any neutral building and start at Phase B.
- **Direction lock** — Move only forward following arrows; pick one path at forks.
- **Kansas City stop** — You may not pass Kansas City; movement ends there.
- **No blocking** — Other cattlemen do not block movement or location use.

#### Player-Count Movement & Fee Values
--- 2 Players ---
- **Initial step limit** — 4.
- **Green hand fee** — $2 each.
- **Black hand fee** — $2 each.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Initial step limit** — 3.
- **Green hand fee** — $2 each.
- **Black hand fee** — $1 each.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Initial step limit** — 3.
- **Green hand fee** — $1 each.
- **Black hand fee** — $2 each.

#### Fee Recipient Rule
- **Hazard or teepee location** — Pay fee to bank.
- **Another player’s private building** — Pay fee to that player.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Phase B Action Families
- **Building actions** — Stop on neutral/private buildings to resolve printed actions.
- **Cattle market actions** — Buy market cattle and improve deck quality.
- **Job market actions** — Hire workers (cowboys, craftsmen, engineers).
- **Railroad actions** — Upgrade train/station progress for scoring and delivery leverage.
- **Hazard actions** — Remove hazards for rewards and VP pathways.
- **Teepee interactions** — Interact with Indian trade section/economic effects tied to teepees.

#### Herd Deck Handling
- **Deck zones** — Draw stack, hand, and face-up discard pile form your herd deck system.
- **Action costs** — Many actions require discarding specific cattle cards from hand.
- **Reshuffle rule** — If you must draw and draw stack is empty, shuffle discard into new draw stack, then continue drawing.

#### Rule Checks
- **Destination legality** — Movement must end at a location, never an empty trail space.
- **Prerequisite legality** — If action requires specific cards/resources/workers, requirements must be met before resolving effects.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Hybrid Route + Deck System
- **Route engine** — Movement path determines available actions and fees paid.
- **Deck engine** — Hand quality at Kansas City drives delivery value.

#### Repeated Delivery Loop
- **Cycle pattern** — Reach Kansas City, deliver by train, then continue future loops from trail start area.
- **Typical cadence** — Players usually reach Kansas City about 5-7 times.

#### Player-Built Board Economy
- **Private buildings** — Players place custom buildings to shape trail actions and interaction.
- **Fee pressure** — Hand-icon fees turn route control into economic pressure.

#### Staged Tile Pipeline
- **Back numbers 1/2/3** — Tile entry is staged for balance.
- **Section feed** — Tiles feed hazard zones, Indian trade, job market, and foresight spaces.

#### Workforce Specialization
- **Cowboys** — Support cattle-focused development.
- **Craftsmen** — Support building-focused development.
- **Engineers** — Support railroad-focused development.

#### Shared Building-Side Variant
- **Advanced setup variant** — Randomized a/b sides copied by all players keep private tile identities aligned across table.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Endgame Summary from Provided Excerpt
- **Scoring timing** — All VP are awarded at game end.
- **Score method** — Add VP symbols on your owned components and your color-marked board elements.
- **Winner** — Highest VP total wins.

#### VP Sources Explicitly Mentioned in Excerpt
- **City deliveries** — Sending cattle by train to cities can grant VP.
- **Private building placement** — Listed as a VP path.
- **Train station upgrades** — Listed as a VP path.
- **Hazard removal** — Listed as a VP path.
- **Objective/component symbols** — Final tally includes printed VP symbols in your area and board marks.

#### Arbitration Note
- **Excerpt limitation** — Provided text is truncated before full end-trigger and official tie-break wording.
- **Practical handling** — For rules arbitration, use complete official rulebook text for exact end trigger/tie process.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early-Game Priorities
- **Loop coherence** — Build first route loop around cash + one engine gain, not disconnected one-offs.
- **Herd quality** — Buy early cattle that improve both value and type diversity.
- **Fee awareness** — Avoid expensive hand-icon crossings unless action upside clearly compensates.
- **Lane commitment** — Choose an early staffing lane (cowboys, craftsmen, or engineers) by your second cycle.
- **Deck discipline** — Keep discard/draw rhythm clean so Kansas City hands stay valuable.

#### Player-Count Pressure Notes
--- 2 Players ---
- **Interaction profile** — Fewer blockers, but green/black fee costs are both $2, so each paid crossing is expensive.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Interaction profile** — Black-hand tolls are cheaper ($1), enabling more tactical passes through black-tax routes.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Interaction profile** — Green-hand tolls are cheaper ($1), but board competition and route congestion pressure increase.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Weak early deliveries** — Rushing Kansas City with low-value, low-variety hands stalls economy.
- **Fee leakage** — Repeatedly paying tolls without checking alternate lines drains cash tempo.
- **Single-axis overfocus** — Investing only in one subsystem (rail/buildings/cattle) can leave scoring gaps.
- **Objective neglect** — Treating objective cards as optional weakens endgame VP ceiling.
- **Bad discard timing** — Dumping key cattle before delivery can collapse payout value.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Major Trade-Offs
- **Tempo vs payload** — Take short loops for more deliveries, or longer loops for stronger action chains and bigger drops.
- **Deck growth vs board growth** — Buy better cattle now, or spend turns on workers/buildings first.
- **Utility vs interaction buildings** — Place private buildings for your own efficiency, or to tax and redirect opponents’ paths.

metadata.notes: Primary source is the provided Great Western Trail rulebook excerpt (components, setup, herd/deck explanation, turn phases, movement/fee rules, and scoring overview). The supplied gameplay text is truncated before full action iconography, formal end trigger, and tiebreak wording; this guide preserves only excerpt-supported specifics and flags missing arbitration details.
